summary: Closed PR intended to merge the fighter branch back into master after resolving merge conflicts. The author stated that the DOL was matching and that remaining cleanup was still necessary, but the slice contains no changed files, diff, reviews, or comments, so exact code changes and technical details cannot be verified from this dump.
what_changed: Author reported that merge conflicts had been fixed on the merge_fighter_master branch.
PR was opened to bring fighter-related work into master.
Author stated that the DOL was matching after the conflict resolution.
No changed files or diff excerpt are present in the PR context, so no concrete source-level changes can be attributed.
smart_moves: Prioritized preserving a matching DOL while resolving branch merge conflicts.
Explicitly noted that additional cleanup remained, distinguishing match-critical integration from later code quality work.
decomp_lessons: For large decompilation branch integrations, a matching DOL can be the critical acceptance signal even when cleanup remains.

When a PR is primarily conflict resolution, reviewers and future agents need the diff or changed-file list to recover useful technical lessons; absent that evidence, avoid inferring symbol, struct, or function-level changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The only matching-related evidence is the author's statement that the DOL was matching after resolving conflicts.
